When someone goes from being a buyer in power, or four years of that, when someone goes from being a buyer in power, right? Meaning they want your product, need your product, but there's no urgency. They're qualified financially, but they're still, they're lacking that itch to scratch, they're not really in pain. And because of that, they're like saying, you know, I'm gonna buy, but when I'm good and ready, when I'm shopping around, if they feel like they're in power, versus the other sort of buy, which is a buyer in heat, your best buyer of all, is someone that needs your product, wants your product and afford your product, but they also are not in denial of their pain. They're feeling the pain actively, and because of that, they need to take action now in their own life, so they're ready to buy. It's urgency, right? So what I really explained to you, which I really want you to focus in on here, is that when you are gathering intelligence, when you're qualifying, not only are you looking to build rapport through active listening, continue that reassure that you are an expert in your field, also obviously sift through those buyers that come into your funnel to take out the ones who are the lookaloo, the tire kickers, right? And the mistakes, so you can take the ones that are those, the buyers need buyers of power and bring those down the line, right? But besides those two things, watch again, sifting out the right buyers, which are the buyers that eat and buyers in power, eliminating the mistakes and the lookaloo, right? That's number one. Number two, building massive rapport at conscious and unconscious level by your active listening, right? So when they respite, uh-huh, mm-hmm, mm-hmm. The third thing is that when you amplify pain as you gather intelligence, what it does is it begins the process of taking a buyer in power and turning them into a buyer in heat, which then by default creates urgency. And what really allowed me to explain so effectively today was a metaphor, an example I used about like how I was in a situation, right? Where I went into my normal checkup, right? And I took a blood test and I get this urgent call from the doctor saying that you have to come into the hospital right now. Your triglycerides are off the charts and your cholesterol is really, your triglycerides are dangerously high. I'm like, oh, really? How high? He goes, well, normal is like 120, you know, 52 under 200, right? High is 200 to 500. 500 to 1,000 is dangerously high. Mine were 5,000. 5,000, it's like, are you walking around right now? Is your pancreas, I'm like, what the fuck, pancreas? Anyway, long story short, I went, panic mode, I call my wife, I gotta go. Huh, I gotta, I'm freaking out, right? I get in the point, the best doctor right away for the next day, right? Or he's like, I had to wait two days a weekend, right? For the best guy ever. Docs like, don't worry, you'll be all right, but you just gotta see a doctor, right? I was so nervous, right? I couldn't get it off my mind for one day. The next day, even though I know it's still there, I block it out, I go into denial, and I live my life again. Why? Because or else I'll drive myself crazy. In other words, what happens is, when someone has a problem, when someone has a lack, someone's experienced something negative in their life, they need to do something, you know, here I am. I'm basically a buyer that comes to the game to the doctor. I'm a buyer, I'm the ultimate buyer, I gotta get things here. I gotta figure out what's wrong with me, I gotta get it to here, I gotta run to the doctor. What could be a more painful buyery than me, right? By Monday, I was like, oh, I really got, I mean, I guess I gotta go, but I had been able to block it off and clump, mentalize it and go into some sort of denial because I need to live my life. You're investors in China, right? People in China, and it's scary for them, so they can't get their money out of the country very easily, right? And when you're gathering intelligence from these people, the question is, you know, a lot of times they'll buy real estate and other parts of it because they're trying to move their money out of the country. So the question is, you know, also, you know, when did you first think about doing this? You know, what did you hear? And say, oh, well, you know, I have a friend of mine who I got and had an issue, I read an article, and they're like, uh-huh, tell me about that. And you start digging into that pain, you see what happens is when they first heard that article, or first heard the story, or read an article, just got that sense that there was problems maybe in the country, that the government could at any time come in and seize their assets, I'm not saying that's gonna happen, but it's a possibility, right? When they first heard it, they panicked. I gotta get my money out of the country, I gotta get real estate elsewhere. But then, over the weeks and months that follow, well, if that was on their mind constantly, they'd be so negative also they couldn't live with themselves. So what do they do? They convince themselves, they go into denial, to convince them, you know what, it's okay, my life is fine as it is, I'll be, they're not gonna take my money, it's gonna be okay. And while that's a healthy self-protective mechanism, it stops them from going nuts and they can live their life and keep working, it doesn't really, certainly do need to still get their money out of the country, they've just gone into denial, because that's how they can live their life, or else if you will only focus on what hurt you, you'd just be going crazy all day. That's why most buyers in power are buyers in power. They once were buyers in heat, but then they become numb, as Pink Floyd says, they're comfortably numb to their own pain. So when you are asking questions at your intelligence gathering session, and you hit on a pain point, what you want to do is take them back to the time when they first realized that there was a problem, say, oh, so what was it that made you think of that, tell me about that, uh-huh, mm, and when was that, ooh, uh-uh, I bet you that was, wow, oh my God, and make them talk about it, bring it back, how'd you feel? I can imagine when you first heard it, I understood now you obviously have to know, but you must have had that feel, tell me, and in a very elegant and sympathetic tone, make them talk about what first caused them to think about making a decision, and what happens is you take them out of denial, back into this position when they're feeling the pain, feeling the discomfort and the worry, and you're doing them a favor, you're not disempowering them, you're empowering them to know that, you know what, I know I got comfort, I gotta do something about this, I gotta make a decision, I can't think of a higher ethical purpose than a salesperson for helping someone who's in denial smell the, you pull your head out of the stand and take a look around, pal, you need to do something, now if you say that like that in the wrong way, you're not gonna get very far, but by using your active listening, by asking good questions and telling them, tell me about that, when did you first find out, oh, wow, how'd that make you feel? Oh my God, I know, I have lots of clients, they all say, uh-huh, I understand you had to keep going, but that must have flipped you, right? Yeah, it's a big issue, and then make them talk. Don't resolve their pain right there, let it stay, and then now as you make your presentation or for your solution, you're now talking to someone who's starting to move towards being a buyer and heat again. In other words, buyers in power buy when interest sale they become buyers and heat. You said something that creates that urgency, that feeling that you know what, I'm gonna do it now, I should be doing it now. Now part of that is when you, there's an equation here, right, the level of urgency is inversely related to how great a fit they think that you'll progress. In other words, the greater they think, the better they think your product is, the less urgency they need to feel to buy it. They could be not that urgent, but you know, it's so great, I'll do it. But it's not always easy to get that super airtight logic, it's to get them that excited about your product, we'll say, you know what, even though I'm not in a rush, this is the one. So what you do is by raising up the pain threshold, okay, there's this inverse, they don't have to be as certain to say yes. That's why it works like the action threshold, the action threshold is the same thing, the lower the action threshold, the less certain someone has to be to say yes.
